one_liner: Nitricity makes distributed, climate-smart nitrogen fertilizer from air, water and renewable electricity.
elevator_pitch: Nitricity makes nitrogen fertilizer from air, water and renewable electricity. Its fertilizer is produced using a plasma-based process without fossil fuels and in a form that can drastically mitigate agricultural emissions from farming fields.
critical_need: Artificial nitrogen fertilizer is the cornerstone of modern society, responsible for feeding our growing population. But for the past century, nitrogen fertilizer has been produced as ammonia (NH3) in facilities using the Haber-Bosch manufacturing process responsible for 1.6 percent of global carbon dioxide emissions. Plus, this ammonia must then be shipped hundreds to thousands of miles to the farms that use it, growing its carbon footprint and the farmer’s costs. Add to this two other hurdles: production costs tied to volatile natural gas prices, and entrenched supply chains that keep the product unobtainable to developing economies. Farmers the world over need a better way to feed their crops.
technology_vision: Nitricity has developed a system for producing nitrogen fertilizer on-demand using just air, water, and electricity. During the fellowship, Nitricity is developing an array of solar-powered fertilizer systems and establishing its product and growth in local California farming communities. For every pound of nitrogen it sells, Nitricity will mitigate approximately four pounds of carbon dioxide emissions by displacing a pound of conventionally-produced and applied nitrogen. Plus, because the fertilizer will be produced onsite, farmers will be empowered to apply nitrogen more frequently, increasing the nitrogen use efficiency of the field. Tailoring nitrogen application in this way has been shown to decrease nitrate runoff by up to 25 percent, which means less loss of product and less pollution of local water supplies.
potential_impact: By using renewable fuel to electrify onsite production of nitrogen fertilizer Nitricity’s vision is to empower farmers to control their own sustainable nitrogen fertilizer supply while also mitigating carbon dioxide emissions, reducing nitrate runoff, and enabling cost-effective nitrogen fertilization anywhere in the world. Within 15 years, Nitricity plans to scale globally with the target goal of mitigating more than 10 million tons of carbon dioxide per year (or ~2 million passenger vehicles each year). In developing regions that currently have limited access to nitrogen fertilizer, Nitricity’s production model will provide a reliable, low-cost, and localized nitrogen source to meet the needs of farmers as they respond to demands from the growing middle class.

name: Radical Plastics
tagline: A radical solution to plastic pollution
one_liner: Radical Plastics blends a patented, naturally-occurring catalyst with conventional polymers to make affordable plastics that are completely biodegradable in the natural environment and recyclable.
elevator_pitch: Radical Plastics has developed a patented catalyst that renders conventional plastics biodegradable in the natural environment. By blending this naturally-occurring catalyst with regular plastic resins, it provides an economical, drop-in replacement solution for existing, persistent plastics. Radical Plastics compounds are easy to process on existing equipment, they have the same properties as conventional plastics and are still fully recyclable so they support the circular economy. But if these materials leak into the environment, they will cleanly and completely biodegrade. Let's reduce, reuse, recycle AND REDESIGN plastics so we can enjoy their many benefits without polluting the planet.
critical_need: Plastics production has surged from 15 million tons in 1964 to 380 million tons in 2017, and it is expected to double again over the next 20 years. While plastics have come to serve an increasing number of applications, they represent a massive pollution problem: millions of metric tons enter our oceans each year, killing sea life and endangering food supplies. This environmental cost is driving demand for plastics that are more ecologically friendly but have the same performance as conventional plastics. Some bioplastics could reduce our reliance on fossil fuel feedstocks, but these materials are prohibitively expensive and often fail to meet performance specifications. Plus, while some are truly biodegradable, others will only decompose into microplastics that persist in the environment.
technology_vision: Radical Plastics compounds have the same molecular structure and properties as conventional plastics, can be cost-competitive with and made on the same equipment as conventional plastics, while remaining recyclable. The key difference is that Radical Plastics will completely biodegrade when triggered by the sunlight, heat, water, air and in the presence of natural microorganisms. In one example, the degradation in soil is expected to happen within two years in compliance with the European Union’s for biodegradable plastic mulch (EN17033:2018). The company’s near-term plan is to address an application that currently inflicts significant business and ecological costs: agricultural mulch films. Farmers spend around $4 billion annually to apply, remove, and dispose of mulch film. Radical Plastics offers a mulch film that would biodegrade offering identical performance with reduced total cost.
potential_impact: Consumers are demanding environmentally compatible packaging however industry has struggled to meet exacting performance and price profiles. Radical Plastics believes its innovation offers a path forward with materials that perform like and can be recycled along with conventional plastic, but which also mitigates the serious impacts of plastic pollution.

name: Resonant Link
one_liner: Resonant Link delivers wireless power for mission-critical applications like medical devices, smartphones, and electric vehicles.
elevator_pitch: Resonant Link delivers breakthrough wireless power for mission-critical applications like medical devices, smartphones, and electric vehicles. Its wireless charging technology lays the foundation for powering electric fleets, and in healthcare its tech can eliminate drivelines and battery replacement surgeries for medical implants.

name: ReSource
tagline: Plastic from CO2 and inedible biomass
one_liner: Resource Chemical's vision is to replace plastic made from petroleum with sustainably-sourced materials that offer superior performance at lower cost.
elevator_pitch: ReSource Chemical is on a mission to replace plastic made from petroleum with sustainably sourced materials that offer superior performance at lower cost. Made from CO2 and inedible biomass using a groundbreaking chemical technology, ReSource products have significant carbon dioxide reduction potential compared to their petroleum-based and conventional bio-based counterparts.
critical_need: The plastics industry is a major contributor to global carbon dioxide emissions because commodity plastics are made from petroleum-derived monomers. Under pressure to both reduce its environmental footprint and improve the performance of existing polymers to capture new markets, the industry seeks new, sustainable feedstocks. The bio-based monomer FDCA, for example, is a well-known, high-performance building block that can serve as a green replacement for PTA, a common petroleum-derived monomer used to make the polyester known as PET. With a market of more than $100B, PET is used in bottling, food packaging, films and fibers. FDCA-derived polymers have superior gas barrier, mechanical and thermal properties, compared to those made from PTA, which can increase the shelf life of packaged food and beverages while using less plastic. But bringing such new performance-advantaged polymers with lower carbon intensity to market, and at scale, requires new process technologies.
technology_vision: Despite a decade of significant industry investment, the process complexity and high production cost of FDCA has frustrated large-scale commercialization. ReSource Chemical has developed a unique approach to producing FDCA—a key ingredient in polyesters, polyurethanes, polyamides, as well as non-toxic plasticizers—with significant advantages over conventional processes. Building on fundamental academic work at Stanford and process scale-up efforts funded by ARPA-E, ReSource Chemical has a radically simplified proprietary chemical technology to make FDCA from carbon dioxide and inedible biomass. This approach requires just a third of the process steps (and commensurate reductions in capital and operating costs) of state-of-the-art, enabling low-cost and potentially carbon-negative production for commodity-scale applications.
potential_impact: ReSource Chemical is on a mission to replace plastic made from petroleum with sustainably sourced materials that offer superior performance at lower cost. Bio-based chemicals made from inedible biomass have significant carbon dioxide reduction potential vis-à-vis their petroleum-based counterparts. In addition, the enhanced performance of FDCA-based plastics in food and beverage packaging applications offers significant secondary climate benefits through reduced food waste, refrigeration costs, transportation costs, land and fertilizer use, water usage and materials consumption. Improved packaging performance also benefits food safety and access, particularly for vulnerable populations.

name: SiTration
tagline: Ultra-durable filtration membranes for energy-efficient industrial separations
one_liner: SiTration is developing a solution for the low-cost sustainable extraction of critical materials in the lithium-ion battery recycling process.
elevator_pitch: SiTration's core technology enables the efficient extraction of lithium, cobalt, and nickel in a hydrometallurgical process. At scale, SiTration aims to reduce the operating cost for critical materials extraction by over 50 percent, while cutting reagent and energy consumption by 10X.

name: Sublime Systems
tagline: An electrochemical pathway to cement production
one_liner: Sublime makes low-carbon cement.
elevator_pitch: Sublime Systems has developed a breakthrough process to make low-carbon cement (currently responsible for 8 percent of global CO2). The technology replaces the industry's legacy fossil-fuel-intensive thermal kiln with an electrochemical process that operates at ambient temperatures with renewable electricity.

name: Sync Computing
tagline: A new chip architecture for optimization problems
one_liner: Sync uses novel mathematical algorithms to instantly and optimally provision cloud resources for today's largest computing workloads.
elevator_pitch: Sync changes the way we interact with today's cloud computing infrastructure, allowing companies and users to choose resources based ONLY on the things that matter most: cost and time. In the process, it dramatically simplifies and lowers the cost of computing in the cloud.

name: Umaro Foods
one_liner: UMARO Foods is making seaweed protein the most abundant, accessible, and affordable protein source on earth.
elevator_pitch: UMARO Foods is making seaweed protein the most abundant, accessible, and affordable protein source on earth. Its first product using seaweed protein is the holy grail of plant-based meat: a delicious crispy, crunchy, bacon.

name: Zanskar
one_liner: Zanskar is a geothermal discovery platform—leveraging big data and advanced seismic imaging to identify and de-risk geothermal resources.
elevator_pitch: Zanskar’s proprietary suite of subsurface imaging tools combines AI, big data, and advanced sensing to pinpoint the most prolific geothermal systems on the globe. Their technology aims to catalyze geothermal energy as a key component of a 100% carbon-free electric grid.

name: ZestBio
tagline: Turning food waste into biobased products
one_liner: ZestBio manufactures and formulates specialty chemicals for water treatment and cleaning applications from industrial fruit and vegetable wastes.
elevator_pitch: Zestbio manufactures chemicals from the perennial 200 billion pounds of peel/pulp byproduct made by the citrus and sugar beet processing industry. Using synthetic biology to derivatize agricultural byproducts, Zestbio produces chemicals for water treatment, cleaning products, and cosmetics that are currently imported from abroad and derived from petroleum-based processes.

name: Aralez Bio
one_liner: Aralez Bio uses engineered enzymes to make valuable chemical products and to expand the scope of products that can be produced bio-renewably.
elevator_pitch: Aralez Bio invented and commercialized an enzymatic platform to synthesize noncanonical amino acids (ncAAs). These unnatural building blocks are a $1.3 billion market and are found in 12 percent of the top 200 grossing pharmaceuticals, with additional applications in synthetic biology and agrochemicals. Despite their utility, ncAAs are extremely challenging to synthesize: state-of-the-art methods are laborious, multi-step procedures that generate 100 times more waste than product, necessitate the mining and import of rare earth metals, and expose scientists to hazardous working conditions. In contrast, Aralez's engineered enzymes can manufacture hundreds of enantiopure ncAAs in a single step, with water as the only byproduct. This technology merges the benefits of industrial enzymes--efficiency, specificity, and waste reduction--with the versatility of chemical manufacturing to provide sustainable routes to useful products.

name: Brimstone
tagline: Cleaner ways to make industry proven materials
one_liner: Brimstone is developing carbon-negative ordinary Portland cement that is capable of reducing greenhouse gas emissions by up to 15 percent.
elevator_pitch: Cement production is responsible for 5.5 percent of global GHG emissions, the same as cars. Brimstone is building a technology that can make the exact same cement product that is on the market today but with negative emissions and at cost parity.

name: Cypris Materials
one_liner: Cypris Materials is developing a new generation of sustainable color that broadens the color gamut while simplifying manufacturing, application and formulation.
elevator_pitch: Cypris Materials is an advanced materials company founded to re-invent humans' relationship with color. Its vision is to bring nature's structural color to the market as a sustainable solution to global energy inefficiencies and alternative to toxic pigments and dyes. This short video shows the formulation and simple application of Cypris' invisible inks which magically form color: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bFQWq-pbwpE

name: InchFab
one_liner: Inchfab is a foundry for beyond-compute chip fabrication that offers ultra-fast cycle time.
elevator_pitch: Inchfab provides rapid AIoT chip manufacturing services. Using a proprietary, AI/ML-driven microfabrication platform, Inchfab reduces customer cycle time by 10x, reducing time to market in addition to development and production costs. To meet the demands of today's environment where computing is moving toward specialized applications which require custom chips, Inchfab's $1M tabletop platform disrupts the existing $BB ecosystem and provides a scalable and economical fabrication solution.

name: Perceptra Technologies
one_liner: Preceptra develops optical analyzers for realtime chemical monitoring in pharmaceutical and medical applications.
elevator_pitch: Preceptra is developing optical analyzers for realtime chemical monitoring. It is applying its sensor platform to a host of applications ranging from biomanufacturing and precision agriculture to health monitoring.

name: Reveel
one_liner: Reveel is disrupting fast fashion. With patent-pending MagicInk™️ personalized apparel is accessible, scalable, and environmentally conscious.
elevator_pitch: Reveel is re-imagining fashion for the digital era. Download, print, and share your designs or NFTs in the real world with Reveel graphic t-shirt printer with color-changing MagicInk, then erase the design and print a new one. Infinite possibilities on one canvas.

name: SkyGig
one_liner: SkyGig is developing advanced mmWave technology for 5G and satellite wireless communications markets.
elevator_pitch: With the rising demands of data intensive applications, there is a need for more advanced wireless technologies to enable the emerging 5G/6G telecommunication infrastructures as well as satellite communications system. Through innovative holistic approaches to the wireless radio technology, SkyGig is bringing a whole new class of wireless systems and architecture to the market that provide significant cost-savings and performance boost for the operators and service providers.

name: Synopic
tagline: A new perspective on smart imaging
one_liner: Synopic is synergistically merging image, machine learning, and visualization technologies to enhance how we understand and interact with the world.
elevator_pitch: 3D cameras are being integrated into many facets of our personal and professional lives, from automotive to entertainment to industry. In healthcare, however, current 3D camera technologies are limited by trade-offs in size, range, and visualization capabilities, limiting their use in many procedures. While many of the 15 million laparoscopic procedures per year enjoy the benefits of 3D imaging, endoscopic surgeries are too space-constrained for current 3D technology. Synopic is developing a new type of compact 3D camera with a single viewpoint, capable of scaling with endoscopes for real-time, high-resolution color imaging for better screening & diagnosis and improved procedure outcomes.

name: terrafuse
tagline: Actionable climate intelligence for a resilient earth
one_liner: Terrafuse is building data fusion technology to re-engineer the climate and weather risk prediction infrastructure around next-generation AI models.

name: ActiveMEMS
tagline: Powering the next generation of the IoT
one_liner: ActiveMEMS is developing advanced microfabrication technology to integrate high-quality piezoelectric materials and transducers into MEMS manufacturing.

name: Antora Energy
one_liner: Antora Energy is electrifying heavy industry with thermal energy storage for zero-carbon heat and power.

name: CinderBio
one_liner: CinderBio's technologies enables new ultra-stable enzymes for industrial and laboratory applications.

name: Fervo Energy
one_liner: Fervo Energy commercializes technology to develop, own, and operate geothermal assets as the dispatchable foundation to a clean energy future.

name: Noble Thermodynamic Systems
one_liner: Noble Thermodynamics’ closed-loop reciprocating engine recycles a noble gas working fluid and captures all carbon dioxide and other pollutants.

name: Noon Energy
tagline: A new class of ultra-low-cost battery
one_liner: Noon Energy’s flow battery technology enables economical long-duration energy storage of renewable energy to meet continual demand.

name: Sonera
one_liner: Sonera is democratizing portable, high-performance functional brain imaging with dramatically reduced size, weight, power consumption, and cost.

name: Takachar
tagline: Harvesting value from agricultural & forest waste
one_liner: Takachar can transform massive amounts of waste biomass, much of which is currently burned, into marketable products around the world.

name: Dauntless.io
tagline: Real-time, adaptive machine learning for energy applications
one_liner: Dauntless.io uses ultra-fast adaptive machine learning algorithms to cost-effectively control complex energy systems with real-time software and hardware.

name: Gradient
one_liner: Gradient (formerly TREAU) is rethinking air conditioners with a new high-efficiency design and low-carbon refrigerants.

name: Nelumbo
one_liner: Nelumbo brings metamaterials (precise surface morphology or surface structure) with extraordinary properties to a wide range of products.

name: Photia
one_liner: Photia is developing technology for industrial-scale manufacturing of 3D nanostructured materials for energy applications.

name: Liminal Insights
one_liner: Liminal’s ultrasound and machine learning analytics platform, EchoStat®, delivers advanced inspection and process control throughout multiple battery manufacturing stages.

name: Mallinda Materials
tagline: Vitrimer matrix composites for the circular economy
one_liner: Mallinda’s infinitely recyclable platform chemistry turns composite materials that were previously unrepairable and unrecyclable into circular products.

name: Sepion Technologies
tagline: Advanced membranes for batteries and water treatment
one_liner: Sepion combines the latest in nanoscience, polymer chemistry, and cell engineering to deliver affordable lithium-metal batteries with disruptive performance.

name: Tandem PV
one_liner: Tandem PV’s mechanically-stacked perovskite and silicon tandem solar panels boost efficiency while reducing manufacturing cost.

name: Tierra Biosciences
one_liner: Tierra Biosciences is accelerating bio-based materials by transforming the way people discover, screen, and develop proteins.

name: CalWave
one_liner: CalWave Power Technologies harnesses the renewable power of ocean waves to produce electricity.

name: Mosaic Materials
tagline: Advanced materials for a cleaner future
one_liner: Mosaic Materials is dedicated to reducing the cost and environmental impact of fossil fuels through the application of proprietary, highly efficient gas separation technologies.

name: PolySpectra
tagline: Functional materials for advanced additive manufacturing
one_liner: polySpectra makes functional materials for advanced additive manufacturing. Its modular platform enables materials with a broad spectrum of tailored properties.

name: Spark Thermionics
tagline: A power plant on a chip
one_liner: Spark Thermionics is building a power plant on a chip: a generator that can efficiently convert any fuel to electricity at 1/10th the size and weight of the state-of-the-art.

name: Twelve
tagline: Carbon transformation for a climate positive world
one_liner: Twelve (formerly Opus 12) transforms carbon emissions into critical chemicals, materials and fuels that today are made from fossil fuels.

name: Visolis
one_liner: Visolis uses synthetic biology and traditional chemical catalysis to produce bio-based chemicals, from drop-in commodity chemicals to innovative specialty offerings.
